visual_notes: Jadeite's waxy polish and semi-transparency in the finest green material ('glassy' or 'icy') is visually unmistakable. The fei-cui terminology in Chinese trade describes a full quality spectrum. A water-drop test (drops stand high on polished surface) and the cold, greasy feel are tactile identifiers used by experienced traders.
prompt_fragments: imperial jade vivid green semi-transparent glow
waxy jadeite polish cool to touch
vibe_notes: Jadeite carries perhaps the heaviest cultural weight of any mineral on Earth — the civilizational stone of China and Mesoamerica simultaneously, formed in the crushing pressure of subduction zones and revered for millennia. Imperial jade's vivid semi-transparent green is one of the most coveted colors in human history.
folklore_summary: In ancient China, the deceased were sometimes buried with jade pieces placed on bodily orifices to preserve the body — jade burial suits (玉衣) of the Han dynasty, made from thousands of jade plaques sewn with gold, silver, or bronze wire, represent the apex of this tradition.
